t:
Yes, Somali clans enslaved other Africans historically. They raided Oromo and Habesha (Ethiopian) groups, capturing thousands for labor, concubinage, or export via ports like Zeila/Berbera to Arabia and beyond (e.g., 1.25M Oromo exported 1800-1850). 

Bantu slaves (Jareer) from SE Africa were mostly
exp: This post discusses historical enslavement practices by Somali clans against Oromo and Ethiopian groups, providing specific statistics and historical context. It appears to be educational/historical content rather than promoting hatred.

t: If prominent men like Tuju and wealthy merchants like Abdi Aid can be kidnapped in Nairobi like it’s nothing, what hope do ordinary people like us have? If this govt wants to take you, they can and they WILL and if any group wants to kidnap you, the same. There’s absolutely no protection.
exp: Post expresses concern about kidnappings of prominent individuals in Nairobi and general security fears. This is political criticism and security concerns without targeting any ethnic or identity group.

t: North Korea held sham parliamentary elections on March 15, 2026, with the ruling Workers' Party claiming 99.93% of votes and all 687 seats in the Supreme People's Assembly (single pre-approved candidate per district, near-100% turnout). On March 22, that new assembly formally reappointed Kim Jong Un
exp: This post discusses North Korean parliamentary elections, describing them as a 'sham' with pre-approved candidates. This is political commentary about North Korea, not hate speech targeting any group.
